scenario: Zenith Bank is sketching the architecture for a Camunda 8.8 process solution that will orchestrate 18000 transactions per day across a payments microservice. An architect must decide how the application will reach the workflow engine before any BPMN is drawn, and wants to align on the two basic options the docs describe for connecting an application to Zeebe.
question: Which pair of options does the best-practices guide describe for connecting an application to Zeebe in Camunda 8?

options.text: Use a JDBC-style driver shipped with the broker for in-process calls or attach to the embedded engine running inside the application JVM and bypass any remote protocol layer
Run the workflow engine inside the application as a library or run it as a sidecar in the same pod and let both modes share the same in-memory event bus for communication
Use BPMN listeners that the broker pushes directly into the application classpath, or rely on a SOAP gateway the broker exposes for legacy systems that cannot consume gRPC
Write programming glue code using the client library for the chosen language, or use an existing connector that just needs configuration to integrate with the target system

a.text: Incorrect. Zeebe is always a remote system for the application and is reached via gRPC or REST; there is no in-process JDBC-style driver or embedded engine in Camunda 8.

b.text: Incorrect. The workflow engine is not embedded into the application in Camunda 8; the docs describe Zeebe as a remote system reached over remote protocols.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs do not describe broker-pushed listeners or a SOAP gateway as connection options; Zeebe is reached over gRPC or REST.

d.text: Correct. The connecting-the-workflow-engine page describes two basic options: write programming code that leverages a client library for the chosen language, or use an existing connector that only needs configuration.

explanation: Zeebe is a remote system for the application and is reached via remote protocols such as gRPC or REST, similarly to how a database is reached via JDBC. The best-practices page lists exactly two options to connect an application to Zeebe: writing programming glue code with the client library, or using an existing connector that only needs configuration.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
